mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.97% indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 1.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above 6.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 6.5% or increases by more than 0.3pp in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 6.5% is elevated relative to pre-pandemic levels
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice growth.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls below 800k annualized units
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 817k is near trigger threshold
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 5-year training window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 107.0 signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reduce equity allocations due to margin compression concerns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 107.0 or declines > 1.5% in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 107.254 is near historical trend
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 200k month-over-month AND UNRATE declines by > 0.2pp in 1 month
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: A rising inflation rate (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2.0%) signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets react to tightening expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.97% is below trigger threshold
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) in triggered instances, or interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained expansion in real GDP (GDPC1) above trend growth of 2.5% signals strong aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of above-trend GDP growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 750k annualized units signals deteriorating housing demand and forward-looking economic weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate earnings downgrades.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 750k annualized units or declines > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 817k is above trigger but near critical threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite housing weakness, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
